title: MLflow Experiment Tracking Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of tracking ML experiments, comparing runs, and managing the model lifecycle with MLflow.

questions.question: In MLflow, what is the difference between a 'Parameter' and a 'Metric'?

options.text: Parameters are logged after training; Metrics are logged before
Parameters are inputs you control before training (hyperparameters); Metrics are output measurements after training (accuracy, AUC)
Parameters store model weights; Metrics store training data
There is no difference; they are synonyms in MLflow
Automatically deploys the best-performing model to production
Automatically captures parameters, metrics, and the model artifact without manual log_param/log_metric calls
Automatically tunes hyperparameters using grid search
Automatically creates a Docker container for the model

questions.question: Why should you use a remote PostgreSQL + S3 backend for MLflow tracking in production instead of the default local ./mlruns folder?

options.text: The local folder does not support logging metrics
PostgreSQL makes model training faster
The local folder doesn't survive server restarts and isn't shareable across a team
S3 is required by law for storing ML models

questions.question: How do you load the 'Production' stage version of a model named 'churn_predictor' from the MLflow Model Registry?

options.text: mlflow.pyfunc.load_model('models:/churn_predictor/Production')
mlflow.load('churn_predictor', stage='production')
joblib.load('mlflow/models/churn_predictor/production.pkl')
mlflow.sklearn.restore_model('churn_predictor')

questions.question: What best practice should you follow when logging dataset information in MLflow?

options.text: Never log dataset information as it increases storage costs significantly
Log dataset version, row count, and a feature hash as run tags to ensure any experiment can be reproduced exactly
Upload the entire raw CSV dataset as an MLflow artifact on every run
Only log the dataset name, not its contents or version
